parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 4 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Init Cursor

### Init Cursor

Shapes the item: writes `page`, `hasMore`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Page Loop

### Page Loop

Loop controller — releases 1 item(s) per pass. Output 2 (loop) processes the batch, output 1 (done) fires after the last batch.

Ports: ①→ Remove Duplicate Requests · ②→ Fetch Page from API

### Fetch Page from API

GET https://api.example.com/v1/requests — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Split Result Items · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Split Result Items

Explodes the `data` array into one item per element.

Next: Normalize Page Items

### Normalize Page Items

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming request records to a stable schema

Next: Page Loop

### Remove Duplicate Requests

Drops duplicates within this run (compared on `requestId`).

Next: Upsert Harvest into HubSpot

### Upsert Harvest into HubSpot

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Ports: ①→ Tally Harvest Stats · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Tally Harvest Stats

Group-by + aggregate (like a pivot table) — turns raw rows into KPI numbers.

Next: Sort Requests Newest First

### Sort Requests Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (HubSpot)

### Append Audit Row (HubSpot)

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#ai-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#ai-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Enrich contact records with HubSpot and HTTP / REST API 24/7 for Multi-Tool Agent (01295)

**Pattern:** Data Enrichment · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Enrich contact records with HubSpot and HTTP / REST API 24/7 for Multi-Tool Agent (01295). It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual multi-tool agent is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Init Cursor → Page Loop → Remove Duplicate Requests → Upsert Harvest into HubSpot → Tally Harvest Stats → Sort Requests Newest First → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to data enrichment without manual effort
- Anyone automating multi-tool agent in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Has retries and error handling built in

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; HubSpot private-app token; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Split In Batches drives the pagination loop instead of a fixed number of HTTP calls, because the page count isn't known upfront — the loop output feeds back into itself until the source stops returning data. Fetching one page per iteration, not all pages at once, keeps memory bounded even against a source with tens of thousands of records.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Change the page-size parameter and watch how many loop iterations it takes to finish.
2. Add a hard cap (a Limit node) so a runaway source can't loop forever.
3. Point the fetch target at a real paginated API and adjust the cursor logic to match its response shape.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your multi-tool agent process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
